pattern: A valid run can only ever start on an even value under the cap, and it snaps the instant parity stops alternating or a value exceeds the cap, so one greedy sweep that restarts at every break finds the longest run directly.
hints: A qualifying subarray must begin on an even number that is itself within the threshold -- so only ever consider starting a new candidate run at such a position.

From a valid start, keep extending to the right only while the next value stays within the threshold and its parity differs from the value right before it; the moment either condition fails, the run is over.

Slide forward and repeat: whenever a run ends, immediately try starting a fresh run at the very next position (it only succeeds if that position is itself a valid even start), tracking the best length seen along the way.

edge_inputs.description: run of length 3 in the middle of the array
single element, even and within threshold
single element that is odd, no valid run at all
every element equals the threshold exactly
an even value appears but exceeds the threshold, cannot start a run there
parity alternates but breaks briefly before resuming, must not merge the two runs

stress.description: 100 random values (max length) with a random threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(size, 1), 100)
    threshold = rng.randint(1, 100)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, threshold]
